message: Proposed data sources and features for the churn target, drawn from approved queries and entity documentation. Important: I flagged 'last_payment_date' as a leakage risk — it's only populated after renewal, so it would secretly encode the answer. I've marked it to exclude.
reasoning: Used approved MJ: Queries and DBAutoDoc to identify engagement and tenure signals; enumerated leakage risks aggressively.

CandidateSources.Why: Approved query with per-member login and activity counts — engagement is a leading churn indicator.
Tenure, plan tier, and demographic attributes available at prediction time.

CandidateFeatures.Name: LoginCountLast30Days
CandidateFeatures.Why: Recent disengagement is an early churn signal.

CandidateFeatures.Name: TenureMonths
CandidateFeatures.Why: Newer members churn at different rates than long-tenured ones.

CandidateFeatures.Name: PlanTier
CandidateFeatures.Why: Tier correlates with commitment and price sensitivity.

LeakageNotes.Risk: Only populated after a renewal occurs, so it directly reveals the outcome — a classic leak that looks great in testing but is unusable in production.
